Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act
Identification.ShortTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code and the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act to, among other things, repeal certain mandatory minimum penalties, allow for a greater use of conditional sentences and establish diversion measures for simple drug possession offences.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 84(5) of the Criminal Code before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In determining, for the purpose of subsection 99(2), 100(2) or 103(2), whether a convicted person has committed a second or subsequent offence, if the person was earlier convicted of any of the following offences, that offence is to be considered as an earlier offence:

In determining, for the purpose of subsection 85(3), 95(2), 99(2), 100(2) or 103(2), whether a convicted person has committed a second or subsequent offence, if the person was earlier convicted of any of the following offences, that offence is to be considered as an earlier offence:

Section.Text: Subsection 85(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1) or (2) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years.
Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1) or (2) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a first offence, except as provided in paragraph (b), to imprisonment for a term not exceeding fourteen years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a second or subsequent offence, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of three years.

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: [Repealed, 2008, c. 6, s. 3]

Section.Text: Subsection 92(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1) or (2) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years.
Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1) or (2) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a first offence, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding ten years;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a second offence, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding ten years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a third or subsequent offence, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding ten years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of two years less a day.

Section.Text: Paragraph 95(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years; or

Subsection.Text: Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a first offence, three years, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a second or subsequent offence, five years; or

Section.Text: Paragraph 96(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years; or

Subsection.Text: Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding ten years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; or

Section.Text: Subsection 99(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In any other case, a person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years.

In any other case, a person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year.

Section.Text: Subsection 100(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In any other case, a person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years.

In any other case, a person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year.

Section.Text: Subsection 103(2.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In any other case, a person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years.

In any other case, a person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year.
Paragraph 121.1(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years; or

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years and, if the amount of tobacco product is 10,000 cigarettes or more or 10 kg or more of any other tobacco product, or the amount of raw leaf tobacco is 10 kg or more,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a second offence, to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of 90 days,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a third offence, to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of 180 days, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a fourth or subsequent offence, to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of two years less a day; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 121.1(5) of the Act is repealed.

For the purpose of determining whether a convicted person has committed a second or subsequent offence, an offence under this section for which the person was previously convicted is considered to be an earlier offence whether it was prosecuted by indictment or by way of summary conviction proceedings.

Section.Text: Paragraph 244(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years.

Subsection.Text: Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of four years.

Section.Text: Paragraph 244.2(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years.

Subsection.Text: Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of an indictable offence and

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of four years.

Section.Text: Paragraph 344(1)(a.1) of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Every person who commits robbery is guilty of an indictable offence and liable

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case where a firearm is used in the commission of the offence, to imprisonment for life and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of four years; and

Section.Text: Paragraph 346(1.1)(a.1) of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Every person who commits extortion is guilty of an indictable offence and liable

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case where a firearm is used in the commission of the offence, to imprisonment for life and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of four years; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 742.1(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the offence is not an offence under any of the following provisions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: section 239, for which a sentence is imposed under paragraph 239(1)(b) (attempt to commit murder),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 269.1 (torture), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 318 (advocating genocide); and

Section.Text: If a person is convicted of an offence and the court imposes a sentence of imprisonment of less than two years, the court may, for the purpose of supervising the offenderâs behaviour in the community, order that the offender serve the sentence in the community, subject to the conditions imposed under section 742.3, if

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the offence is not an offence, prosecuted by way of indictment, for which the maximum term of imprisonment is 14 years or life;
Â

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the offence is not an offence, prosecuted by way of indictment, for which the maximum term of imprisonment is 10 years, that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: resulted in bodily harm,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: involved the import, export, trafficking or production of drugs, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: involved the use of a weapon; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the offence is not an offence, prosecuted by way of indictment, under any of the following provisions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: section 144 (prison breach),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 264 (criminal harassment),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 271 (sexual assault),

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: section 279 (kidnapping),

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: section 279.02 (trafficking in persons â material benefit),

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: section 281 (abduction of person under fourteen),

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 333.1 (motor vehicle theft),

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 334(a) (theft over $5000),

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 348(1)(e) (breaking and entering a place other than a dwelling-house),

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: section 349 (being unlawfully in a dwelling-house), and

Subparagraph.Label: (xi)
Subparagraph.Text: section 435 (arson for fraudulent purpose).

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 742.1(e) and (f) of the Act are repealed.

Section.Text: Paragraph 5(3)(a) of the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the subject matter of the offence is a substance included in Schedule I or II, is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for life;

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes subsection (1) or (2)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the subject matter of the offence is a substance included in Schedule I or II, is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for life, and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year if

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the person committed the offence for the benefit of, at the direction of or in association with a criminal organization, as defined in subsection 467.1(1) of the Criminal Code,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the person used or threatened to use violence in committing the offence,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the person carried, used or threatened to use a weapon in committing the offence, or

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: the person was convicted of a designated substance offence, or had served a term of imprisonment for a designated substance offence, within the previous 10 years, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of two years if

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the person committed the offence in or near a school, on or near school grounds or in or near any other public place usually frequented by persons under the age of 18 years,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the person committed the offence in a prison, as defined in section 2 of the Criminal Code, or on its grounds, or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the person used the services of a person under the age of 18 years, or involved such a person, in committing the offence;

Section.Text: Paragraphs 6(3)(a) and (a.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the subject matter of the offence is a substance included in Schedule I or II, is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for life;

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes subsection (1) or (2)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the subject matter of the offence is a substance included in Schedule I in an amount that is not more than one kilogram, or in Schedule II, is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for life, and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the offence is committed for the purposes of trafficking,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the person, while committing the offence, abused a position of trust or authority, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the person had access to an area that is restricted to authorized persons and used that access to commit the offence;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: if the subject matter of the offence is a substance included in Schedule I in an amount that is more than one kilogram, is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for life and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of two years;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 7(2)(a) and (a.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the subject matter of the offence is a substance included in Schedule I or II, is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for life;

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the subject matter of the offence is a substance included in Schedule I, is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for life and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of three years if any of the factors set out in subsection (3) apply and for a term of two years in any other case;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: if the subject matter of the offence is a substance included in Schedule II, is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for life, and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for a term of one year if the production is for the purpose of trafficking, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for a term of 18 months if the production is for the purpose of trafficking and any of the factors set out in subsection (3) apply;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 7(3) of the Act is repealed.
The following factors must be taken into account in applying paragraphs (2)(a) and (a.1):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person used real property that belongs to a third party in committing the offence;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the production constituted a potential security, health or safety hazard to persons under the age of 18 years who were in the location where the offence was committed or in the immediate area;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the production constituted a potential public safety hazard in a residential area; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the person set or placed a trap, device or other thing that is likely to cause death or bodily harm to another person in the location where the offence was committed or in the immediate area, or permitted such a trap, device or other thing to remain or be placed in that location or area.

Section.Text: Section 8 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 18:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of the heading and section 8:

Section.Text: The court is not required to impose a minimum punishment unless it is satisfied that the offender, before entering a plea, was notified of the possible imposition of a minimum punishment for the offence in question and of the Attorney Generalâs intention to prove any factors in relation to the offence that would lead to the imposition of a minimum punishment.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 10(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
If a person is convicted of a designated substance offence, the court imposing sentence on the person shall consider any relevant aggravating factors including that the person

If a person is convicted of a designated substance offence for which the court is not required to impose a minimum punishment, the court imposing sentence on the person shall consider any relevant aggravating factors including that the person
Subsection 10(5) of the Act is repealed.
If the offender successfully completes a program under subsection (4), the court is not required to impose the minimum punishment for the offence for which the person was convicted.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 10:
The following principles apply in this Part:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: problematic substance use should be addressed primarily as a health and social issue;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: interventions should be founded on evidence-based best practices and should aim to protect the health, dignity and human rights of individuals who use drugs and to reduce harm to those individuals, their families and their communities;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: criminal sanctions imposed in respect of the possession of drugs for personal use can increase the stigma associated with drug use and are not consistent with established public health evidence;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: interventions should address the root causes of problematic substance use, including by encouraging measures such as education, treatment, aftercare, rehabilitation and social reintegration; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: judicial resources are more appropriately used in relation to offences that pose a risk to public safety.

Subsection.Text: A peace officer shall, instead of laying an information against an individual alleged to have committed an offence under subsection 4(1), consider whether it would be preferable, having regard to the principles set out in section 10.1, to take no further action, to warn the individual or, with the consent of the individual, to refer the individual to a program or to an agency or other service provider in the community that may assist the individual.
The failure of a peace officer to consider the options set out in subsection (1) does not invalidate any subsequent charges laid against the individual for the offence.

Section.Text: A prosecution may be commenced or continued against an individual alleged to have committed an offence under subsection 4(1) only if, having regard to the principles set out in section 10.1, the prosecutor is of the opinion that the use of a warning or referral under section 10.2, or of alternative measures as defined in section 716 of the Criminal Code, is not appropriate, and a prosecution is appropriate in the circumstances.

The police force to which a peace officer referred to in section 10.2 belongs may keep a record of any warnings or referrals relating to individuals alleged to have committed an offence under subsection 4(1).

Section.MarginalNote: Evidence of warning or referral not admissible
Section.Text: Evidence that an individual has received a warning or referral mentioned in subsection 10.2(1), evidence that a peace officer has taken no further action in respect of an offence under subsection 4(1) and evidence of the offence are inadmissible for the purpose of proving prior offending behaviour in any proceedings before a court in respect of the individual.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (2) to (11) apply if Bill C-21, introduced in the 2nd session of the 43rd Parliament and entitled An Act to amend certain Acts and to make certain consequential amendments (firearms) (in this section referred to as the other Act), receives royal assent.
If paragraph 14(a) of the other Act comes into force before section 4 of this Act, then that section 4 is amended by replacing the subsection 95(2)(a) that it enacts with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years; or

Subsection.Text: If section 4 of this Act comes into force on the same day as paragraph 14(a) of the other Act, then that section 4 is deemed to have come into force before that paragraph 14(a).
If paragraph 14(b) of the other Act comes into force before section 5 of this Act, then that section 5 is amended by replacing the paragraph 96(2)(a) that it enacts with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years; or

Subsection.Text: If section 5 of this Act comes into force on the same day as paragraph 14(b) of the other Act, then that section 5 is deemed to have come into force before that paragraph 14(b).
If paragraph 14(c) of the other Act comes into force before section 6 of this Act, then that section 6 is amended by replacing the subsection 99(3) that it enacts with the following:
In any other case, a person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years.
If section 6 of this Act comes into force on the same day as paragraph 14(c) of the other Act, then that section 6 is deemed to have come into force before that paragraph 14(c).
If paragraph 14(d) of the other Act comes into force before section 7 of this Act, then that section 7 is amended by replacing the subsection 100(3) that it enacts with the following:
In any other case, a person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years.
If section 7 of this Act comes into force on the same day as paragraph 14(d) of the other Act, then that section 7 is deemed to have come into force before that paragraph 14(d).
If paragraph 14(e) of the other Act comes into force before section 8 of this Act, then that section 8 is amended by replacing the subsection 103(2.1) that it enacts with the following:
In any other case, a person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years.
If section 8 of this Act comes into force on the same day as paragraph 14(e) of the other Act, then that section 8 is deemed to have come into force before that paragraph 14(e).
